name: Apollo 11
left: Descent stage · ALSEP · laser retroreflector · flag · plaque: 'We came in peace for all mankind'
fact: The first humans on the Moon. Watched live by ~600 million people — a fifth of humanity. The laser retroreflector placed by Armstrong and Aldrin still receives pulses today, measuring the Earth-Moon distance to millimetre precision. Footprints in the airless regolith will survive unchanged for a million years.
capability: First human landing — proved crewed planetary surface operations are possible. The capability ladder for Mars begins here.
